license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bwcipaue
title: Human embryonic gonad dissociation with Collagenase & Trypsin
author: Carmen Sancho

steps_raw.instruction: Heat-inactivate FBS at 56 °C for 00:30:00 before use. Collagenase mix recipe: %s %s
Digestion mix recipe: %s %s
Wash tissue with PBS.
Place wet tissue under a petri dish. Take 2 scalpels and roughly mince up the tissue. This step is crucial to increase the efficiency of the digestion.
Transfer contents to 15ml falcon containing the collagenase mix (~ 10 mL /tissue but it will depend on the size of the tissue.
Tighten lid and then seal with parafilm.
Incubate at 37 °C for 00:45:00 . Shacking every 10 min during the incubation is recommended.
Filter sample through small strain (100 um) and keep the filter.
Centrifuge 450 g, 00:05:00 (0.5 rcf, 5mins), 4 degrees
Remove carefully 90% of the media by decanting the tube (leave between 0.5 ml and 1 ml).
Resuspend the cell pellet in 2 ml of PBS and pass it through the inverted filter adding extra 3 mL of PBS
Centrifuge 450g, 5 min, 4 degrees
Remove carefully 90% of the media by decanting the tube (leave between 0.5 ml and 1 ml) and transfer to a 1mL eppendorf
Centrifuge 450 g, 00:05:00 (0.5 rcf, 5mins) and discard supernatant
Add 700 microL Trypsin-EDTA/DNase I and Incubate at 37 °C for 10 min with rotation.
Add 700 microL RPMI complete and filter trhough a Facs tube passing 1mL more of media.
Transfer to a 5 mL eppendorf and Centrifuge 450 g, 00:05:00 (0.5 rcf, 5mins)
Discard supernatant without disrupting the cell pellet and with 1 mL of PBS-BSA 0.04%, transfer to a 1,5 mL eppendorf
Centrifuge 450 g, 00:05:00 (0.5 rcf, 5mins) and leave 200-300 microL
Proceed to cell count with disposable C-chip haematocytometer.
